<!-- Generated by GitHub Copilot on 2026-03-29 -->
# Plan: Baoh Assistant

> Source PRD: Presale assistant feature request approved in conversation on 2026-03-29

## Architectural Decisions

Durable decisions that apply across all phases:

- **Routes**: Keep the existing chat entry in the WebUI and repurpose it for `baoh-assistant`, while preserving the current session ID system-message pattern.
- **Schema**: Add a new MCP service `InterviewCoach.Mcp.PresaleData` with normalized `PresaleLead` and `DiscoveryQuestion` tables.
- **Key models**: Move `ConversationPhase` and `RequestType` into `InterviewCoach.Shared`; introduce a presale lead aggregate that stores summary, contact details, transcript, and request classification.
- **Workflow shape**: Use a handoff workflow with `introductionAgent`, `triageAgent`, `discoveryAgent`, `contactCollectionAgent`, `summariserAgent`, and `emailAgent`.
- **State management**: WebUI generates the session ID and passes it as a system message; triage reads persisted state and routes accordingly. `introductionAgent` runs only for new sessions.
- **Knowledge source**: CV and services JSON files live with the Agent project and are loaded into workflow instructions at runtime.
- **Service boundaries**: Persistence remains behind MCP. The `emailAgent` has a dedicated local tool for writing exported lead JSON files into `leads`.
- **Abuse control**: Discovery is hard-limited to exactly 5 questions maximum before transitioning to contact collection.

---

## Phase 1: Establish Shared Presale Contracts

**User stories**: US-1, US-2

### What to Build

Create the shared domain contract for the presale experience so the agent workflow, MCP server, and WebUI all depend on the same durable conversation state and request classification types. This slice also removes chat-local ownership of those enums so later work builds on stable cross-project types.

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `InterviewCoach.Shared` contains the shared `ConversationPhase` and `RequestType` enums.
- [ ] Shared presale types compile cleanly and can be referenced by Agent, WebUI, and MCP projects.
- [ ] Existing chat-local enum ownership is removed or redirected to the shared types.
- [ ] The solution still builds after introducing the shared contracts.

---

## Phase 2: Add Presale Persistence Through MCP

**User stories**: US-1, US-3, US-4

### What to Build

Add a dedicated presale MCP server that can create, update, and resume lead sessions. The slice should store lead-level state in a `PresaleLead` record and discovery Q&A as normalized child records so the workflow can resume across turns and later produce structured exports.

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] New MCP project `InterviewCoach.Mcp.PresaleData` exists in the solution.
- [ ] The MCP server exposes normalized `PresaleLead` and `DiscoveryQuestion` persistence.
- [ ] The MCP tool surface supports creating a lead, retrieving a lead, updating a lead, adding discovery Q&A, and reading discovery Q&A.
- [ ] The persisted lead stores phase, request type, transcript, summary, and client contact fields.
- [ ] The persisted discovery records can be queried independently of the lead row.
- [ ] The MCP server is wired to the existing SQLite-backed local environment.

---

## Phase 3: Build the Baoh Assistant Workflow

**User stories**: US-1, US-2, US-3, US-4, US-5

### What to Build

Implement the new `BaohAssistant` handoff workflow in the Agent project. This slice introduces the extracted presale tool service, loads Bao's CV and services knowledge into the agent instructions, creates the six-agent workflow, and adds the new runtime mode so the app can choose this orchestration path explicitly.

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `AgentMode` supports a dedicated `BaohAssistant` mode.
- [ ] `introductionAgent` greets new clients, introduces Baoh Consulting services, and asks the client to pick an initial service direction.
- [ ] `introductionAgent` initializes the lead record with `RequestType.Unknown`.
- [ ] `triageAgent` routes using persisted session state instead of guessing from free text.
- [ ] `discoveryAgent` refines and persists request classification during discovery.
- [ ] The extracted presale tool service replaces the old in-component presale logic and writes through MCP or other external services.
- [ ] Discovery stops after exactly 5 questions maximum and then transitions to contact collection.
- [ ] The workflow can resume an in-progress session using the same session ID.

---

## Phase 4: Generate and Export Structured Leads

**User stories**: US-4, US-5, US-6

### What to Build

Add the closing workflow behavior that produces a structured client-facing summary, stores that summary in the persisted lead record, and then exports the final lead as a JSON artifact for follow-up. This keeps summarisation and file export as separate responsibilities.

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `summariserAgent` produces a structured summary and presents it back to the client in chat.
- [ ] `summariserAgent` updates the lead record with the summary before handing off.
- [ ] `emailAgent` writes a JSON lead file to the local `leads` folder.
- [ ] Exported file names follow `{timestamp}_{company}_{requestType}.json`.
- [ ] Exported JSON contains summary, contact details, request type, transcript context, and asked discovery questions with answers.
- [ ] The export step does not assume email delivery; it only creates the JSON artifact.

---

## Phase 5: Replace the Stateful Chat with a Thin Shell

**User stories**: US-1, US-2, US-3, US-4, US-5, US-6

### What to Build

Refactor the user-facing chat experience so the existing chat components remain in place, but the business logic moves entirely into the workflow. The WebUI should stay responsible for session ID generation, message presentation, and attachment handling, while the workflow owns phase state, persistence, and structured presale behavior.

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The existing chat entry uses the new workflow rather than in-component presale state management.
- [ ] The chat surface remains built from the current chat component set.
- [ ] The WebUI continues generating and sending the session ID as a system message.
- [ ] Attachments and existing chat input behavior continue to work.
- [ ] The user sees the final structured summary in the chat before the workflow completes.
- [ ] Old in-component presale tracking logic is removed from the UI shell.

---

## Phase 6: Verify End-to-End Behavior

**User stories**: All

### What to Build

Validate the new assistant with focused automated coverage and real local verification of the runtime wiring. This slice proves the workflow can persist, resume, classify, rate-limit discovery, collect contact information, summarise, and export leads without regressing the current solution structure.

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Focused tests cover workflow routing, shared type usage, and lead export behavior.
- [ ] The app host wires `InterviewCoach.Mcp.PresaleData` into the Agent and WebUI stack.
- [ ] Build succeeds from the repository root using the documented solution command.
- [ ] A real local run confirms startup without new initialization failures unrelated to missing secrets or remote connectivity.
- [ ] Resuming a session with the same session ID continues from the persisted phase.
- [ ] Discovery cannot exceed 5 questions in a single lead flow.
